name: nezam-testing-strategy
description: Unit, integration, E2E, and visual regression strategy with Playwright/Cypress/Vitest, mocking, and test data discipline.
version: 1.0.0

---

# Purpose

Define the testing pyramid: unit (Vitest/Jest), integration (API/component), E2E (Playwright/Cypress), visual regression (Loki/Chromatic), contract tests (Pact), and mock/test-data discipline. Single-responsibility: test strategy.

# Inputs

- API contract from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-api-design/SKILL.md`.
- Component contracts from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-component-library-api/SKILL.md`.
- A11y plan from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-a11y-automation/SKILL.md`.
- Pipeline structure from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-devops-pipeline/SKILL.md`.

# Step-by-Step Workflow

1. Pyramid sizing: ~70% unit, ~20% integration, ~10% E2E; document rationale per module.
2. Choose tools: Vitest for unit (Vite-native), Testing Library for components, Playwright for E2E (preferred for cross-browser), MSW for HTTP mocks.
3. Author test data factories (e.g., `@faker-js/faker` + builder pattern); avoid shared global fixtures.
4. Contract tests: Pact for consumer/provider verification; OpenAPI schema validation in CI.
5. Visual regression: Storybook + Chromatic or Playwright screenshots with masking; baseline review per PR.
6. Coverage thresholds per module: e.g., 80% statement / 70% branch; document carve-outs.
7. CI ordering: fast feedback first (lint, type, unit, contract); E2E + visual in parallel matrix.

# Validation & Metrics

- Coverage thresholds enforced in CI.
- E2E suite runtime < 15 min on main pipeline.
- Flake rate < 1% (track per spec; quarantine flakes promptly).
- MSW intercepts assert request shape, not just URL.
- Visual baselines reviewed and approved per PR.

# Output Format

- `docs/specs/TESTING_STRATEGY.md` (pyramid, tools, thresholds).
- Test data factory catalogue.
- Playwright config + project matrix.
- Contract test catalogue.
- Flake quarantine policy.

# Integration Hooks

- `/SCAN code a11y perf` runs scoped suites.
- `/DEVELOP` builds against acceptance tests (without writing app code here).
- Pairs with `@.cursor/skills/nezam-api-design/SKILL.md`, `@.cursor/skills/nezam-component-library-api/SKILL.md`, `@.cursor/skills/nezam-a11y-automation/SKILL.md`, `@.cursor/skills/nezam-devops-pipeline/SKILL.md`.
- Honors `[.cursor/rules/workspace-orchestration.mdc](.cursor/rules/workspace-orchestration.mdc)`.

# Anti-Patterns

- E2E suite that re-tests every component (slow, brittle).
- Snapshot tests that gate every PR with no semantic value.
- Mock servers diverging from real API contract.
- Coverage gaming (hitting thresholds with smoke tests).
- Letting flakes pile up (quarantine without owner).

# External Reference

- Playwright (https://playwright.dev/) — current.
- Vitest (https://vitest.dev/) — current.
- Testing Library (https://testing-library.com/) — current.
- MSW (https://mswjs.io/) — current.
- Pact (https://pact.io/) — current.
- Chromatic (https://www.chromatic.com/) — current.
- Closest skills.sh/official analog: testing-strategy / e2e.
